steps.title: Set up and clear the fraction
steps.content:
The area of a triangle is $\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}$. Since Area = $x^2$, base = $2x + 22$, and height = $x - 10$:

$$x^2 = \frac{1}{2}(2x + 22)(x - 10)$$

Multiply both sides by 2 to eliminate the fraction:

$$2x^2 = (2x + 22)(x - 10)$$

steps.title: Expand and solve
steps.content:
Distribute to expand the right side:

$$(2x + 22)(x - 10) = 2x^2 - 20x + 22x - 220 = 2x^2 + 2x - 220$$

So the equation becomes:

$$2x^2 = 2x^2 + 2x - 220$$

Subtract $2x^2$ from both sides—the quadratic terms cancel:

$$0 = 2x - 220$$

Divide by 2:

$$x = 110$$
